title: DailyRounds & Marrow : Building India’s Medical Education Ecosystem with ₹363 Cr Profit in FY'25
content:
Introduction

Bengaluru-based healthcare edtech platform DailyRounds delivered an outstanding performance in FY25, reporting ₹363 crore in profit on ₹641 crore operating revenue. At a time when many edtech companies are struggling with declining growth, high customer acquisition costs, and uncertain business models, DailyRounds stands out as one of India’s most profitable and disciplined digital education businesses. Its success underscores the strength of focusing on a niche, high-value segment with persistent demand, strong willingness to pay, and low churn. By targeting medical students and professionals — a community that continuously requires updated knowledge — the company has built a durable and defensible business.

 

Financial Performance

DailyRounds’ operating revenue grew 13% from ₹568 crore in FY24 to ₹641 crore in FY25, while net profit also increased by 13% year-on-year to ₹363 crore. In addition to core business income, the company generated ₹132 crore from interest on deposits and investments, pushing total revenue to ₹773 crore. The firm reported an EBITDA margin of 57.29% and a Return on Capital Employed (ROCE) of 21.21%, highlighting exceptional operational efficiency. On a unit basis, the company spent just ₹0.46 to earn one rupee of operating revenue — a rare achievement in the edtech sector. These numbers reflect strong pricing power, disciplined spending, and a scalable subscription model that does not rely heavily on aggressive discounting.

 

From Beginning Till Now: How They Began

Founded in 2014, DailyRounds initially started as an online platform where doctors could discuss real clinical cases and share practical insights from their practice. This community-driven approach helped build trust among medical professionals. Over time, the founders identified a major gap in structured preparation for postgraduate medical entrance examinations. Recognizing this unmet need, they launched Marrow, a comprehensive digital learning platform featuring high-quality video lectures, extensive question banks, mock tests, and performance analytics. This strategic pivot transformed DailyRounds from a peer discussion forum into a full-scale edtech powerhouse with recurring subscription revenue and nationwide reach.

 

Founders and Leadership

DailyRounds was founded by medical professionals Dr. Deepu Sebin, Dr. Nimmi Cherian, and Dr. Priyaank Choubey. Their firsthand experience navigating India’s demanding medical education system enabled them to design a product tailored to real student needs rather than generic test preparation formats. Their clinical backgrounds also helped establish credibility within the medical community, which proved critical for early adoption. Under their leadership, the company has maintained a strong focus on content quality, academic rigor, and long-term value creation rather than short-term growth hacks.

 

Cost Structure

On the expense side, legal and professional fees emerged as the largest cost component at ₹91 crore, followed by employee benefit expenses of ₹85 crore. Technology infrastructure costs — including web hosting, payment gateways, advertising, and business promotion — contributed significantly to total expenditure of ₹295 crore in FY25, up from ₹225 crore in FY24. Despite rising costs, the company preserved strong profitability due to its capital-light model, digital delivery, and absence of large physical infrastructure. This lean structure allows DailyRounds to scale efficiently while maintaining healthy margins.

 

Product Expansion and Market Position

Marrow remains the primary revenue engine, contributing approximately 88% of operating income through subscription plans ranging from three to 36 months. Additional revenue streams include book sales worth ₹75 crore and market research services. Over the years, DailyRounds has established a dominant position in India’s medical postgraduate preparation segment, benefiting from strong brand recognition, high user engagement, and powerful word-of-mouth referrals among students. The platform’s continuous content updates and exam-focused approach create high switching costs for users, strengthening customer retention.

 

Expansion and Growth Outlook

With total current assets of ₹1,826 crore — including ₹1,756 crore in cash and bank balances — DailyRounds is exceptionally well positioned for future growth. This strong balance sheet provides flexibility to invest in product innovation, global expansion, or strategic acquisitions. However, the rapid emergence of AI-driven learning tools presents both opportunities and challenges. To maintain leadership, the company will need to integrate advanced technologies while preserving the credibility and depth of its content. Given the stable demand for medical education, the long-term outlook remains positive.

 

Conclusion

DailyRounds’ journey demonstrates how deep domain expertise, a focused target market, and disciplined execution can build a highly profitable startup even in a challenging sector. For founders and investors alike, it offers a compelling blueprint for sustainable scaling in specialized education markets. As India’s healthcare ecosystem expands, platforms like DailyRounds are likely to play a crucial role in shaping the next generation of medical professionals.

title: Success Story "Zoho" - A Bootstrapped Global Tech Giant Built on Independence and Innovation
content:
Introduction

Every transformative company begins with a belief that challenges conventional wisdom. Zoho is a remarkable example of how long-term thinking, deep engineering, and financial discipline can create a world-class technology enterprise without external funding. Founded in 1996 (originally as AdventNet) by Sridhar Vembu and his co-founders, Zoho has grown into one of the world’s most comprehensive SaaS providers, serving businesses in over 150 countries while remaining privately owned, profitable, and fiercely independent.

 

Founder Background & Idea Origin

Sridhar Vembu, an IIT Madras graduate with a PhD from Princeton University, began his career in the United States but envisioned building a global technology company rooted in India. The company started as AdventNet, developing network management tools for telecom equipment providers.

As the internet economy expanded, the founders recognized a larger opportunity: businesses worldwide needed affordable, integrated software delivered through the cloud. This insight led to the creation of Zoho’s suite of online business applications, beginning with CRM and expanding into finance, HR, collaboration, analytics, and IT management tools.

 

The Market Gap

In the early 2000s, enterprise software was dominated by expensive, complex systems from global vendors. Small and medium-sized businesses faced high licensing costs, fragmented tools, and heavy infrastructure requirements. Many organizations simply could not afford digital transformation.

Emerging markets faced an even bigger challenge — a lack of locally built enterprise-grade solutions tailored to their needs. Zoho identified this underserved global segment and positioned itself as a provider of powerful yet affordable cloud software accessible to businesses of all sizes.

 

The Solution: A Unified Cloud Operating System for Business

Zoho’s strategy was to build a deeply integrated ecosystem rather than isolated products. Today, the company offers more than 50 applications covering sales, marketing, finance, customer support, HR, collaboration, and operations. This unified approach reduces complexity, lowers costs, and improves efficiency for customers.

A defining principle of Zoho’s philosophy is ownership of the entire technology stack. The company operates its own data centers, builds its own development tools, and prioritizes privacy by avoiding ad-driven revenue models. This independence allows Zoho to innovate continuously while maintaining control over performance and security.

 

Bootstrapped Growth & Financial Discipline

Unlike most technology startups, Zoho has never raised venture capital. It has relied entirely on internal profits to fund expansion, research, and global operations. This bootstrapped model has allowed the company to focus on long-term customer value rather than short-term investor expectations.

As of early 2025, Zoho generated over $1.3 billion in annual revenue and was valued at nearly ₹50,000 crore (around $6 billion). The company has remained consistently profitable for more than 25 years — a rare achievement in the SaaS industry.

Zoho also reinvests heavily in research and development and strategically acquires companies aligned with its technological goals, including ventures in artificial intelligence and semiconductor innovation.

 

Growth Momentum in 2025–2026

Recent years have marked a period of accelerated expansion. In 2025, Zoho reported 20 percent year-on-year revenue growth and a 32 percent increase in customers. The company crossed a major milestone in early 2026, surpassing one million paying customers and 150 million users worldwide.

This growth has been driven by strong adoption among small and medium businesses, rising demand for cloud-based operations, and new AI capabilities such as the launch of Zia LLM. Internal projections suggested that Zoho could approach $2 billion in annual revenue in 2026, reinforcing its position as one of the largest privately held software companies globally.

 

Unconventional Organizational Strategy

Zoho’s success is also rooted in its distinctive organizational philosophy. Rather than concentrating operations in expensive urban centers, the company has expanded into smaller towns and rural areas, including regions around Chennai and Tenkasi. This approach, often described as “transnational localism,” focuses on nurturing local talent while maintaining global reach.

By decentralizing operations, Zoho reduces costs, improves employee retention, and contributes to regional economic development. The company also invests in training programs to develop skilled engineers from non-traditional backgrounds, further strengthening its talent pipeline.

 

Business Model & Competitive Position

Zoho operates on a subscription-based SaaS model that generates predictable recurring revenue. Its competitive advantage lies in offering enterprise-grade functionality at accessible prices while maintaining deep product integration.

The company’s limited reliance on advertising and sales spending contrasts sharply with many competitors. Instead, Zoho emphasizes product quality, customer support, and organic growth through word-of-mouth. This disciplined approach has enabled sustainable scaling without sacrificing profitability.

 

Global Impact & Recognition

Zoho has empowered millions of organizations worldwide to digitize operations, improve productivity, and compete globally. Its success demonstrates that world-class technology companies can emerge outside traditional innovation hubs and thrive without external funding.

In India, Zoho represents a model of self-reliant entrepreneurship — combining technological excellence with social impact through rural job creation and skill development. Its story challenges the assumption that venture capital is essential for building global software leaders.

 

Future Vision

Looking ahead, Zoho plans to deepen its investments in artificial intelligence, automation, and integrated business platforms while expanding further into emerging markets. The company remains committed to its founding principles of independence, customer focus, and long-term sustainability.

 

Conclusion & Takeaway

Zoho’s journey proves that patient growth, strong engineering culture, and financial discipline can build enduring global enterprises. From a small network tools provider to a software powerhouse serving over 150 million users, the company has rewritten the rules of startup success.

By staying private, profitable, and customer-centric for three decades, Zoho demonstrates that true innovation is not just about rapid scale — it is about building technology that lasts. In an era dominated by venture-funded growth stories, Zoho stands as a powerful reminder that independence can be the ultimate competitive advantage.


Behind many transformative companies are ecosystems that support founders with the right mentorship, capital, and strategic guidance. If you are building the next global technology success story — or looking to invest in India’s innovation future — Angeltors is where vision meets opportunity.

title: Coulomb Litech Raises ₹20 Crore in Seed Funding to Accelerate Heavy-Duty EV Battery Innovation
content:
Introduction

India’s electric mobility revolution is expanding beyond passenger vehicles into commercial and industrial segments, where reliable battery technology is critical for large-scale adoption. Medium and heavy electric vehicles — including tractors, construction machinery, and logistics equipment — demand high-performance batteries capable of fast charging, long operational life, and consistent performance under tough conditions. One emerging player addressing this challenge is Coulomb Litech, a Mumbai-based startup developing advanced battery systems tailored for heavy-duty EV applications. The company has now secured fresh capital to scale manufacturing and strengthen its presence across India’s rapidly growing EV ecosystem.

 

About the Funding

Coulomb Litech has raised ₹20 crore in a seed funding round from high-net-worth individuals (HNIs). The investment reflects increasing confidence in indigenous battery technology companies that can support India’s transition toward electrified transportation and energy systems. As commercial fleet electrification accelerates, startups capable of delivering durable, high-capacity battery solutions are attracting early-stage capital. This funding will help Coulomb Litech move from pilot deployments to scaled production and broader market adoption.

 

Use of Funds

The newly raised capital will primarily be used to establish a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Navi Mumbai. The company also plans to deploy funds toward business expansion, research and development, and scaling ongoing pilot programs across heavy electric vehicle segments. Additional investments will support working capital needs and strengthen collaborations with OEM partners and fleet operators. By increasing manufacturing capacity and improving product reliability, Coulomb Litech aims to accelerate the commercialization of its battery solutions across industrial and commercial use cases.

 

About the Startup

Founded in 2020, Coulomb Litech focuses on designing and manufacturing fast-charging, long-life battery systems specifically for medium and heavy electric vehicles. Unlike consumer EV batteries, which prioritize compact size and passenger comfort, commercial EV batteries must withstand intensive usage cycles, heavy loads, and challenging environmental conditions.

The startup develops battery solutions for applications such as material handling equipment, electric tractors, construction machinery, cranes, electric three-wheelers (E-3W), and stationary energy storage systems. These segments represent a massive opportunity, as industries seek cleaner alternatives to diesel-powered machinery while maintaining operational efficiency.

 

How It Started and the Founders

Coulomb Litech was founded by Ameya Sathe and Darshil Dharod, both alumni of IIT Kanpur. The founders identified a major gap in the availability of robust battery solutions for heavy-duty electric mobility in India. While passenger EV adoption was gaining momentum, commercial vehicles faced limitations due to insufficient battery performance, long charging times, and durability concerns.

With a strong engineering background, the founders set out to build a fully integrated battery technology platform developed entirely in-house, enabling greater control over performance, safety, and cost.

 

Technology and Market Opportunity

Coulomb Litech’s battery systems operate across a wide voltage range of 51V to 700V, with capacities reaching up to 80 kWh. This flexibility allows the company to serve diverse applications, from smaller utility vehicles to large industrial equipment. Its modular battery platforms — spanning medium-voltage and high-voltage systems — are designed to meet the demanding operational requirements of commercial EVs.

A key differentiator for the startup is its vertically integrated technology stack. Coulomb Litech has developed its hardware, software, and algorithms internally, including Battery Management Systems (BMS), liquid cooling solutions, CCS fast-charging systems, and IoT-enabled analytics platforms. This integrated approach supports rapid charging while extending battery lifespan — a crucial factor for fleet operators seeking lower total cost of ownership.

The market opportunity is substantial. As governments and industries push toward decarbonization, electrification of logistics, agriculture, construction, and public transport is expected to accelerate. Reliable battery partners will play a central role in enabling this transition.

 

Business Model and Revenue Streams

Coulomb Litech operates primarily as a B2B technology supplier to OEMs, fleet operators, and industrial equipment manufacturers. Revenue is generated through the sale of battery systems, integrated power solutions, and associated software services. The company is already engaged in multiple commercial deployments and pilot projects with OEM partners across India.

In addition to direct product sales, the startup may benefit from long-term supply agreements, maintenance contracts, and data-driven services enabled by its IoT analytics platform. This diversified revenue approach can help create stable recurring income as adoption scales.

 

Future Plans

Looking ahead, Coulomb Litech aims to significantly expand manufacturing capacity, strengthen OEM partnerships, and scale deployments across heavy electric vehicle categories. The company is also focused on enhancing product reliability, safety, and performance consistency under demanding operating conditions.

Localization of battery manufacturing is another key priority, aligning with India’s broader push for domestic production and reduced import dependence. By building a robust supply chain and expanding production capabilities, Coulomb Litech seeks to become a leading battery technology partner for commercial electrification in India and potentially global markets.

 

Conclusion

Coulomb Litech’s ₹20 crore seed funding marks an important milestone in its journey to power the next phase of India’s electric mobility transformation. With strong in-house technology, a focus on heavy-duty applications, and growing partnerships with OEMs, the startup is well positioned to address one of the biggest bottlenecks in commercial EV adoption — reliable, high-performance batteries. As industries transition toward cleaner energy solutions, companies like Coulomb Litech could play a pivotal role in enabling sustainable, large-scale electrification.

title: Building a Capital-Efficient Lifestyle Tech Brand with ₹320 Cr ARR
content:
Introduction

Homegrown D2C lifestyle tech brand DailyObjects is steadily emerging as one of India’s most capital-efficient consumer startup success stories. From its early days selling curated phone cases to building a diversified portfolio of tech accessories, bags, charging solutions, and everyday carry essentials, the company has crossed ₹320 crore in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR). At a time when many direct-to-consumer brands rely on heavy funding and aggressive discounting to grow, DailyObjects has taken a different path — focusing on design ownership, premium positioning, and disciplined expansion. With clear visibility on margins and operational efficiency, the company is now targeting EBITDA profitability by FY26, marking a crucial milestone in its growth journey.

 

Financial Performance and Growth

DailyObjects reported revenue of approximately ₹110 crore in FY25, up from ₹84 crore in FY24, reflecting steady year-on-year growth despite challenging market conditions. However, in its pursuit of scale, losses widened to ₹16 crore from ₹10 crore in the previous year. Procurement costs accounted for the largest expense component at ₹51.5 crore, representing about 41% of total expenses. Advertising spend rose sharply to ₹26 crore, while employee benefit expenses increased to ₹17 crore as the company expanded operations. Despite these rising costs, the brand has demonstrated strong momentum, projecting FY26 revenue of ₹230–244 crore alongside EBITDA positivity. On a unit basis, the company spent ₹1.13 to earn one rupee in FY25, a slight improvement over the previous year.

 

From Beginning Till Now : How They Began

Founded in 2012 by Pankaj Garg and Saurav Adlakha, DailyObjects began as an online platform offering customized and design-led phone cases. Recognizing a growing demand for functional yet aesthetically appealing everyday products, the founders gradually expanded into a broader “lifestyle tech” category. Instead of chasing rapid diversification, the company deepened its presence in adjacent segments such as bags, wallets, stationery, and charging solutions. This focused expansion helped build strong brand recall while maintaining product coherence. Over more than a decade, DailyObjects has evolved from a niche accessory seller into a full-fledged design-first consumer brand with nationwide reach.

 

Founders and Leadership

Under the leadership of Pankaj Garg and Saurav Adlakha, DailyObjects has consistently emphasized long-term sustainability over short-term hypergrowth. Their strategy centers on building intellectual property through in-house design, maintaining tight control over product development, and ensuring premium positioning. This disciplined approach has allowed the company to scale with relatively modest funding — approximately $12–14.5 million to date — far lower than many D2C peers operating at similar revenue levels.

 

Cost Structure and Operational Discipline

DailyObjects’ cost structure reflects typical D2C dynamics but with stronger control mechanisms. Procurement remains the largest expense, followed by marketing and employee costs. Rent, logistics, and professional fees also contribute to overall expenditure as the brand expands offline presence. Importantly, the company has focused on improving operational efficiency over time, resulting in better unit economics even while losses increased during the scaling phase. Its current assets stood at ₹87 crore, including ₹8 crore in cash reserves, providing a buffer for ongoing expansion.

 

Product Expansion and Market Position

Unlike conventional accessory brands, DailyObjects positions itself as a “Lifestyle Tech” company. Every product is designed in-house, following an “aesthetic utility” philosophy that combines functionality with premium design. Products typically undergo an 8–10-month development cycle, resulting in differentiated offerings that command higher pricing. For example, certain power banks are priced significantly above mass-market alternatives yet continue to perform strongly due to perceived quality and design value. This IP-led strategy acts as a competitive moat, protecting the company from commoditization.

 

Omni-Channel Strategy and Bharat Penetration

DailyObjects has adopted a balanced omni-channel approach. Approximately 70% of sales come through owned digital channels, enabling higher margins and direct customer relationships, while the remaining 30% comes from marketplaces for scale and discovery. The company has also expanded its offline footprint through Exclusive Brand Outlets, many of which have reportedly achieved profitability quickly. Travel retail locations, particularly airport terminals, have shown strong performance, highlighting demand from premium consumers. Additionally, partnerships with Apple Authorised Retail stores — currently around 200 locations — are expected to double in the near term. Notably, 40–45% of demand now originates from Tier 3 and Tier 4 cities, indicating that premium lifestyle consumption is expanding beyond metropolitan markets.

 

Expansion and Growth Outlook

Having crossed ₹320 crore in ARR, DailyObjects is entering a new phase focused on profitability and scale. The company aims to achieve EBITDA positivity by FY26 through improved margins, stronger offline economics, and growth in owned channels. Corporate gifting and B2B partnerships are emerging as additional revenue streams. However, the competitive landscape is intensifying due to influencer-led discovery and price comparisons across platforms. To sustain growth, the brand will need continued innovation, sharper positioning, and operational discipline.

 

Conclusion

DailyObjects’ journey demonstrates that sustainable growth in India’s D2C sector is possible without excessive capital burn. By combining design-led differentiation, intellectual property ownership, omni-channel execution, and measured expansion, the company is quietly rewriting the playbook for consumer startups. For founders, investors, and business enthusiasts, this story highlights how disciplined strategy can outperform aggressive funding-led growth.

title: HireBound Raises $2 Million to Transform Hiring with Agentic AI
content:
Introduction

The global recruitment landscape is undergoing a structural shift as artificial intelligence moves from experimentation to real-world execution. Companies today face intense pressure to hire faster, reduce costs, and identify high-quality talent across distributed workforces. Traditional hiring processes—often manual, fragmented, and time-consuming—are increasingly proving inadequate for modern business needs. Addressing this gap, Bengaluru-based startup HireBound is building an AI-first recruitment platform designed to automate the entire hiring lifecycle. The company has now secured fresh capital to accelerate product development and global expansion.

 

About the Funding

HireBound has raised $2 million in a seed funding round led by Kalaari Capital, with participation from Antler, Infinyte Club, and CareerNet. The investment highlights growing investor confidence in AI platforms that can deliver measurable efficiency gains in enterprise processes. As organizations increasingly adopt automation for sourcing, screening, and engagement, recruitment technology has become a high-potential category within enterprise software.

 

Use of Funds

The newly raised capital will be deployed across three strategic priorities. First, HireBound plans to accelerate product innovation by enhancing its agentic AI engine to autonomously manage larger portions of the recruitment lifecycle. Second, the company will strengthen go-to-market operations, including sales, delivery, and customer success functions. Third, funds will support international expansion, with the startup aiming to grow its presence across global markets while adapting to regional hiring regulations and compliance requirements. The company is targeting a fivefold increase in annual recurring revenue (ARR) over the next 12–18 months.

 

About the Startup

Founded by Sharad Vij and Kumar Vikramaditya, HireBound builds an AI-powered recruitment platform designed to function as an “execution layer” rather than a traditional applicant-tracking system. While conventional ATS tools primarily store candidate data, HireBound’s platform actively performs hiring tasks—from sourcing and outreach to screening, evaluation, and candidate recommendations.

The startup’s agentic AI system is built to operate with minimal human intervention, continuously learning from hiring signals and feedback loops. According to the company, its platform has already powered more than 3 million candidate conversations and has been trained on over 2 million resumes and 30,000 job openings. This data foundation enables the system to deliver interview-ready candidates while significantly reducing recruiter workload.

 

Technology and Differentiation

HireBound’s core innovation lies in its autonomous AI agents capable of managing complex, high-context recruitment tasks. The platform integrates seamlessly with existing ATS and HRMS systems, allowing organizations to adopt the technology without replacing their current infrastructure. It is used by staffing firms and enterprise talent teams seeking to scale hiring across multiple locations and roles.

Unlike rule-based automation tools, HireBound’s system is designed to learn and improve over time. It analyzes engagement patterns, hiring outcomes, and performance indicators to refine candidate matching and recommendations. This approach positions the platform as a decision-support and execution engine rather than merely an administrative tool.

 

Market Opportunity

The recruitment industry represents a massive global market, historically dependent on manual processes and human judgment. As companies expand across geographies and remote work becomes mainstream, the need for scalable hiring solutions has intensified. AI-driven recruitment platforms promise faster time-to-hire, improved candidate experience, and reduced operational costs—key metrics for modern talent teams.

Industry analysts expect strong growth in AI adoption across hiring functions throughout the decade. However, challenges such as algorithmic bias, data privacy, and regulatory compliance remain critical considerations. Platforms that can demonstrate transparency, fairness, and measurable improvements in hiring outcomes are likely to gain a competitive advantage.

 

Business Model and Traction

HireBound operates primarily as a B2B SaaS platform serving staffing agencies, recruitment partners, and enterprise clients. Revenue is generated through subscription fees, managed hiring services, and potentially value-added analytics offerings. The company reports growing traction among enterprise talent teams, validating demand for AI-driven hiring solutions that go beyond traditional record-keeping systems.

Despite operating with a relatively small team of around 15 members across product, AI/ML, engineering, and go-to-market functions, HireBound aims to scale rapidly. Planned hiring across engineering, operations, and customer success will support the next phase of growth.

 

Investor Perspective

Investors view HireBound as addressing a structural gap in high-velocity, multi-location hiring. Jayraj Bharat Patel of Kalaari Capital noted that the startup’s agentic architecture and recruiter-first design position it well to redefine how modern hiring teams operate. As enterprises increasingly seek automation that delivers tangible business outcomes, platforms capable of executing complex workflows autonomously are attracting significant interest.

 

Future Outlook

Looking ahead, HireBound plans to expand into international markets over the next 12–24 months, tailoring its platform to local compliance frameworks and hiring norms. The company’s long-term vision is to evolve from a hiring copilot into a comprehensive talent-intelligence layer capable of managing recruitment across roles, industries, and geographies.

 

Conclusion

HireBound’s $2 million seed funding marks an important milestone in the evolution of AI-driven recruitment technology. By combining autonomous execution, data-driven insights, and seamless integration with existing systems, the startup aims to transform hiring from a manual administrative function into an intelligent, scalable workflow. As organizations worldwide compete for talent in an increasingly digital economy, platforms like HireBound could play a central role in shaping the future of work.Top of Form

title: Success Story “Cultfit” : Building India’s Integrated Health & Fitness Ecosystem
content:
Introduction

Every transformative company begins with a vision to change everyday behavior at scale. Cultfit’s journey is a powerful example of how technology, community, and consumer insight can redefine an entire industry. Founded in 2016 by Mukesh Bansal and Ankit Nagori under the parent brand Cure.fit, the company set out to make fitness accessible, engaging, and sustainable for urban Indians. What started as a chain of boutique fitness centers has evolved into a comprehensive health platform spanning gyms, digital workouts, mental wellness, sportswear, and healthy food.

 

Founder Background & Idea Origin

Mukesh Bansal, founder of Myntra and former head of commerce at Flipkart, teamed up with Ankit Nagori, an IIT graduate and ex–Flipkart executive, to tackle a growing lifestyle crisis. They observed that while awareness about health was increasing, consistent fitness habits were still rare. Traditional gyms were often intimidating, monotonous, and poorly managed, leading to low retention rates.

The founders envisioned a new model — one that would combine technology, community motivation, and premium experiences to make fitness enjoyable rather than obligatory. Their goal was not just to open gyms, but to build a holistic health ecosystem.

 

The Market Gap

Before Cultfit, India’s fitness industry was highly fragmented. Most gyms operated independently with inconsistent quality, outdated equipment, and limited professional training. Consumers faced several challenges: lack of motivation, rigid memberships, overcrowded facilities, and little guidance on overall wellness.

Digital fitness solutions were also limited, and few brands offered an integrated approach combining physical workouts, nutrition, and mental health. This gap created an opportunity for a scalable, tech-enabled platform capable of delivering consistent experiences across locations.

 

The Solution: Fitness as a Service

Cultfit introduced a completely new concept — group-based, trainer-led workouts delivered in a studio format. Sessions such as strength training, yoga, boxing, dance fitness, and high-intensity interval training were designed to be fun, structured, and results-driven. The subscription model allowed members to attend classes across multiple centers, providing flexibility that traditional gyms lacked.

Over time, the company expanded into a full-stack health platform. It launched digital workout programs, meditation sessions, and personalized coaching through its app. Additional verticals included Cultsport (sportswear and equipment) and Eat.fit (healthy meals), creating an integrated lifestyle brand rather than a single-service provider.

 

Early Challenges & Strategic Pivots

Building a network of physical fitness centers required significant capital, operational expertise, and real estate management. Cultfit had to ensure standardized training quality, maintain equipment, and manage high fixed costs. Educating consumers about a subscription-based model was another hurdle in a price-sensitive market.

The COVID-19 pandemic posed an existential threat as physical centers shut down nationwide. However, the company responded swiftly by accelerating its digital transformation. Online classes, home workout programs, and equipment sales surged, allowing Cultfit to stay connected with customers and even expand its reach beyond metro cities.

 

Funding Journey & Investor Confidence

Cultfit attracted strong backing from prominent global investors. Early funding came from venture firms such as Accel and Kalaari Capital, followed by major investments from Temasek. In 2021, Tata Digital acquired a majority stake, providing strategic support and integration opportunities within the Tata ecosystem.

These investments enabled expansion across India, technology development, acquisitions of smaller fitness chains, and diversification into new business lines. The backing of trusted institutional investors also strengthened the brand’s credibility in a competitive market.

 

Growth & Revenue Momentum

Cultfit has expanded into dozens of cities, particularly major urban hubs like Bengaluru, Mumbai, and Delhi. Its hybrid model — combining offline centers with digital offerings — allows scalable growth while reducing dependence on physical infrastructure alone.

Revenue streams now include membership subscriptions, personal training, merchandise, digital programs, and partnerships. The brand has also built a strong community of loyal users who view fitness as a lifestyle rather than a short-term activity.

 

Competitive Landscape

India’s fitness sector has seen rising competition from traditional gym chains, boutique studios, and digital fitness apps. However, Cultfit differentiates itself through its integrated ecosystem, technology platform, standardized experiences, and strong brand identity. Instead of competing solely on price, it emphasizes value, convenience, and community engagement.

 

Business Model & Strategy

Cultfit operates on a subscription-driven model designed for long-term retention rather than one-time transactions. Its strategy focuses on creating recurring engagement through varied classes, gamification, progress tracking, and personalized recommendations.

The company’s digital-first approach enables data-driven decision-making, helping tailor offerings to user preferences. Partnerships with corporate clients, residential communities, and health insurers further expand its reach.

 

Impact & Industry Influence

Cultfit has played a significant role in normalizing fitness as a daily habit among India’s urban population. By making workouts social and structured, it has helped reduce entry barriers for beginners while maintaining appeal for experienced users. The company has also created thousands of jobs across training, operations, technology, and retail.

Beyond physical health, Cultfit promotes holistic well-being, including mental fitness and nutrition, aligning with global trends toward preventive healthcare.

 

Future Vision

Looking ahead, Cultfit aims to deepen its digital capabilities, expand into new geographies, and enhance personalization through data and AI. The long-term vision is to become a comprehensive health platform serving millions of users across physical and virtual channels.

 

Conclusion & Takeaway

Cultfit’s journey demonstrates that solving lifestyle problems requires more than a single product — it demands an ecosystem. From reinventing the gym experience to building a multi-vertical wellness brand, the company has transformed how Indians approach health and fitness.

In an era of sedentary lifestyles and rising health concerns, Cultfit stands as a powerful example of how innovation, community, and strategic execution can create lasting impact. It is not just a fitness company — it is a movement toward healthier living at scale.

title: Spintly Raises $8 Million in Series A to Power the Future of Smart Building Security
content:
Introduction

As enterprises modernize infrastructure, physical security and access control systems are undergoing a major digital transformation. Traditional wired access systems are often expensive, complex to install, and difficult to scale across multi-location facilities. With the rise of IoT, cloud computing, and AI-driven analytics, organizations are now seeking flexible, mobile-first, and cloud-managed solutions. Addressing this shift, Spintly — a Goa-headquartered proptech and smart building startup — has secured fresh capital to accelerate global expansion and product innovation.

 

About the Funding

Spintly has raised $8 million in a Series A funding round led by Accel. The round also saw participation from Enrission India Capital, SucSEED Ventures, Alumni Ventures, and Chakra Growth Fund.

The fundraise signals strong investor confidence in wireless infrastructure solutions that replace legacy physical security systems. Prior to this round, Spintly had raised capital from investors including LetsVenture, Riso Capital, and Accel Nest, steadily building momentum in the smart building ecosystem.

 

Allocation of Funds

According to the company, 60% of the new capital will be directed toward growth initiatives, including expanding sales and marketing operations across India, the Middle East & Africa (MEA), and the United States. Another 20% will support research and development, particularly AI-driven smart building capabilities, while the remaining 20% will strengthen operational infrastructure and global expansion efforts.

The funding will also help accelerate product development and enhance enterprise-focused solutions targeting organizations managing access across large commercial spaces and multi-site facilities.

 

Founders and Vision

Founded in 2020 by Rohin Parkar and Malcolm D’Souza, Spintly was built on the vision of simplifying and modernizing physical access control. The founders recognized that traditional wired systems were costly, rigid, and inefficient for rapidly scaling businesses.

Their goal was to develop a wireless, cloud-based platform that could eliminate complex wiring while offering secure, scalable, and remotely managed access control. By combining IoT hardware with intelligent software, Spintly aims to create an integrated smart building ecosystem rather than just an access management tool.

 

Technology and Differentiation

At the core of Spintly’s innovation is its proprietary BLE (Bluetooth Low Energy) mesh network architecture, protected by seven patents. This wireless mesh system replaces conventional wired access control infrastructure, significantly reducing installation time, retrofit complexity, and deployment costs.

Spintly’s platform supports smartphone-based keyless entry, attendance tracking, and visitor management. Its hardware portfolio includes Bluetooth and NFC-enabled readers, QR-code scanners, and biometric systems such as facial recognition and fingerprint devices.

A key advantage of the platform is its ability to store access logs locally during power outages, ensuring continuity and reliability. Additionally, Spintly is integrating AI-driven intelligence into existing camera infrastructure to enhance security analytics, making facilities smarter and more responsive.

The company follows a vertically integrated model — designing hardware in-house while manufacturing through contract partners in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu. Revenue is generated through hardware sales combined with recurring software subscription fees, creating a balanced hardware-plus-SaaS business structure.

 

Market Presence and Traction

India remains Spintly’s largest market, with over 500 customers. The company also serves nearly 100 clients in the United States and has recently expanded into the Middle East, supporting multiple enterprise clients in the region.

Its solutions are deployed across corporate offices, technology parks, co-working spaces, and multi-location enterprises. As businesses increasingly prioritize secure, contactless, and remotely manageable access systems, demand for wireless cloud-based infrastructure continues to grow.

The broader proptech and smart building market is expanding rapidly as organizations seek operational efficiency, enhanced security, and better analytics-driven decision-making. Wireless access control, in particular, is emerging as a foundational layer for next-generation intelligent facilities.

 

Strategic Outlook

Spintly aims to position its wireless mesh architecture as a global standard for enterprise access control and smart building ecosystems. By expanding internationally and strengthening AI-led product capabilities, the company plans to move beyond access management toward comprehensive intelligent infrastructure solutions.

The integration of edge AI, IoT, and cloud analytics places Spintly at the intersection of proptech, security technology, and enterprise SaaS — sectors witnessing strong global investment momentum.

 

Conclusion

Spintly’s $8 million Series A round marks a significant milestone in its growth journey. With strong investor backing, patented wireless technology, and a global expansion roadmap, the startup is well-positioned to disrupt legacy access control systems. As enterprises worldwide modernize physical infrastructure, companies like Spintly are redefining how buildings are secured, managed, and optimized in the digital age.

title: Wint Wealth : Revenue Jumps 2.6X in FY25, Losses Narrow by 60%
content:
Introduction

Bengaluru-based wealthtech platform Wint Wealth delivered a strong financial performance in FY25, marking a significant step toward sustainable scale. The company recorded a 2.6X jump in operating revenue while narrowing its losses by over 60% to ₹8.2 crore. At a time when India’s wealthtech sector is witnessing cautious funding and sharper scrutiny on profitability, Wint Wealth’s performance signals disciplined growth in a segment traditionally dominated by banks and large financial institutions.

Founded in 2020, the six-year-old startup enables retail investors to access fixed-income investment products such as corporate bonds, securitised debt instruments, and non-convertible debentures (NCDs). It also operates a B2B lending arm through its NBFC subsidiary, Wint Capital, further strengthening its revenue base.

 

Financial Performance

According to filings with the Registrar of Companies (RoC), Wint Wealth’s operating revenue surged to ₹44.5 crore in FY25 from ₹17.2 crore in FY24. The sharp rise in scale played a crucial role in reducing net losses to ₹8.2 crore during the same period.

The company’s primary revenue driver is interest income from debt securities and loans issued through Wint Capital, calculated using the effective interest rate (EIR) method. This segment contributed 69% of total operating revenue and grew 3.9X year-on-year to ₹30.8 crore in FY25.

In addition, Wint Wealth generated ₹9 crore from fee-based financial intermediary services, including facilitating debt investments and bond transactions. Another ₹4.7 crore came from net gains on trading debt securities in the secondary market. Interest income from current investments and other non-operating sources added ₹2.3 crore, taking total income to ₹46.8 crore for the fiscal year

.

Founders and Leadership

Wint Wealth was founded by Ajinkya Kulkarni (Co-founder & CEO), Shashank Chimaladari (Co-founder & CTO), Abhik Patel (Co-founder & CPO), Anshul Gupta (Co-founder), and Vinay Dubey (Co-founder & CMO).

Ajinkya Kulkarni leads the company’s strategic vision and frequently represents the firm in public forums. Shashank Chimaladari heads technology, ensuring robust platform infrastructure and compliance-ready systems. Abhik Patel drives product innovation, focusing on simplifying debt investments for retail users. Anshul Gupta plays a key founding role in business operations, while Vinay Dubey oversees marketing and brand positioning.

Together, the founding team combines financial services expertise with technology-driven execution, enabling Wint Wealth to democratize access to fixed-income investments.

 

Cost Structure and Operational Efficiency

Despite strong revenue growth, expenses also rose as the company scaled operations. Total expenditure increased by 32% to ₹54.7 crore in FY25 from ₹41.5 crore in FY24.

Employee benefit expenses remained the largest cost component, accounting for 49% of overall costs and rising 25.6% to ₹27 crore, including ₹4.7 crore in ESOP expenses. Interest expenses formed another major chunk, representing 34% of total costs and jumping 4.4X to ₹18.6 crore as lending operations expanded.

Other overheads included advertising, administrative, and legal expenses. On a unit basis, Wint Wealth spent ₹1.23 to earn one rupee of operating revenue in FY25, reflecting improving efficiency compared to earlier years.

As of March 2025, the company reported current assets of ₹296 crore, including ₹35 crore in cash and bank balances, providing financial stability for continued expansion.

 

Funding and Market Position

Wint Wealth has raised approximately $60 million to date. Its most recent ₹250 crore ($28 million) funding round was led by Vertex Ventures, with participation from Eight Roads Ventures, Zerodha-backed Rainmatter, and 3one4 Capital. The backing from marquee investors strengthens its credibility in India’s competitive wealthtech landscape.

According to Entrackr data, Indian wealthtech startups collectively raised over $634 million across 51 deals during 2024–25. However, large-ticket rounds remain limited, indicating a more selective funding environment. In this context, Wint Wealth’s performance stands out as a sign of operational discipline and investor confidence.

 

Growth Outlook

As retail participation in debt markets increases, Wint Wealth is well-positioned to capture rising demand for alternative fixed-income products. Its dual revenue model — interest income and fee-based services — creates diversification and resilience. Continued focus on compliance, technology infrastructure, and investor education will be critical for long-term growth.

With losses narrowing sharply and revenues accelerating, the company appears to be moving steadily toward sustainable profitability.

 

Conclusion 

Wint Wealth’s FY25 performance highlights how focused execution, diversified revenue streams, and disciplined cost management can drive meaningful progress toward profitability in the wealthtech space. As India’s retail investing ecosystem matures, platforms like Wint Wealth are playing a crucial role in expanding access to fixed-income opportunities.
